response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned web and application developer with over 15 years of experience, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The most critical disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement listed as native-level — which alone eliminates candidacy. Beyond that, his technical background is centered on TypeScript, Node.js, and web platforms, with only a brief 6-month stint writing C at National Instruments. There is no evidence of Linux kernel development, embedded networking, security feature implementation (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), Layer-2/3 networking, or switch/chip-level work.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts demonstrates strong general engineering maturity
Exposure to low-latency systems at Sociomantic (async I/O, high-performance databases) shows awareness of performance-critical programming
Linux is listed as a skill and appears across multiple roles, suggesting baseline familiarity with the OS environment
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — the role requires native-level Japanese, which is a non-negotiable hard requirement
No demonstrable experience in embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication, Layer-2/3 protocols, or switch chip SDKs)
C programming experience is minimal and dated (approx. 6 months at NI in 2014–2015); the role demands strong, ongoing C proficiency for UNIX/embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain knowledge
